title: Easy: D'Alembert's principle
description: Answer all questions. Each carries 1 mark.

questions.title: D'Alembert's principle is a statement of which branch of physics?
Who is credited with formulating D'Alembert's principle?
D'Alembert's principle generalises which static principle to dynamics?
According to D'Alembert's principle, the sum of real and which other forces is zero?
In the equation form of D'Alembert’s principle Σ(F - ma)=0, what does 'ma' represent?
D'Alembert's principle allows a dynamic problem to be transformed into what type of problem?
Which of the following is the fictitious force introduced in D'Alembert's principle?
D'Alembert's principle applies when constraints depend on which quantity?
Which law of motion is equivalent to D'Alembert's principle when inertial forces are included?
If forces and inertial forces produce zero virtual work for any virtual displacement, the system is in what state?
D'Alembert's principle, when applied to a rigid body, makes it behave like what system?
Which term is often used to refer to the added opposing force in D'Alembert's principle?
D'Alembert’s principle does not apply for which type of displacement?
Which of the following is a necessary condition for applying D'Alembert's principle?
options: Virtual work of forces equals zero

questions.title: In D'Alembert's principle, what is the role of acceleration?
options: It is added to applied forces

questions.title: D'Alembert's principle was later written in variational form by which mathematician?
The mathematical expression of D'Alembert's principle involves virtual displacements δr. What are these?
When using D'Alembert’s principle, which force is subtracted from real forces for equilibrium?
D'Alembert's principle simplifies dynamics by converting motion analysis into which other analytical method?
In a system where D'Alembert's principle applies, what does zero total virtual work imply?
options: No net work by real + inertial forces
